The Lotus plant (Nelumbo spp.) is a type of aquatic plant that is native to Asia, known for its fragrant flowers and edible rhizomes. The rhizomes of the Lotus plant have been traditionally used in herbal medicine for their potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of the Lotus plant include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with the Lotus plant include teas, tinctures, and capsules, which are used to support digestive health and reduce inflammation.
taxonomy: Lotus floridus is a species of the genus Lotus, which belongs to the family Fabaceae. The genus Lotus is a group of flowering plants in the legume family.
